key_insight: A neural-network-based time integrator can replace costly plasticity state updates while preserving the plastic consistency condition exactly.
engineering_relevance: This can reduce the cost of nonlinear finite element simulations for inelastic materials without sacrificing stability or accuracy.
method: Train neural networks to predict state-variable updates for a plasticity model.
Use invariants as inputs and determine evolution directions analytically from training data.
Enforce exact plastic consistency during the update step.
evidence_signal: The approach was trained on only 10 cycles of multiaxial proportional loading and then tested in both material-point and finite-element simulations, where it showed good accuracy, stability, and lower runtime.
watch_item: Performance is demonstrated on a prototype von Mises model with nonlinear kinematic hardening, so broader validation on more complex loading paths and material laws is still needed.
paper_title: Machine Learning-Accelerated Time Integration of Plasticity Models
